rulings.Case Title: CATALINO CARINO et al VS. ALIREZA KATOUZIAN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 29, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT BRISTOL-MYERS SQUIBB COMPANY's Amended Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Summary Judgment is denied. Triable issues of fact exist as to whether Defendant had a duty to warn of the risks of paralysis and paraplegia associated with epidural injections of particulate corticosteroids. Inadequate drug warnings cannot be the legal cause of injury when the undisclosed risks are already known, either by the prescribing physician personally (Rosburg v. Minnesota Mining & Mfg. Co. (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 726, 735) or to the medical profession generally (Plenger v. Alza Corp. (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 349, 362.) Here, Defendant failed to produce evidence that Dr. Katouzian was aware of the risks associated with the use of particulate as opposed to non-particulate corticosteroids. Further, both Defendant and Plaintiff presented opposing expert opinions as to whether the risks were well known within the medical community. (See Ferrante Decl. 16, 17 [risks are well-known in the medical community]; Gart Decl. 7 [the risk of neurological injury with the use of Kenalog was not universally known or obvious].) Additionally, triable issues exist as to whether the warnings were adequate. Manufacturers have a duty to warn of risks of off-label uses if the manufacturer knew of the off-label use. (Miles Laboratories, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1982) 133 Cal.App.3d 587, 595-96.) Defendant has not produced evidence sufficient for the Court to determine as a matter of law that the warning was adequate, but instead this is a question for the trier of fact. Summary adjudication of the claim for negligent manufacturing defect is granted as Plaintiff does not oppose. Summary adjudication is granted for the claim of negligent design defect because Defendant is entitled to judgment as a matter of law. (See Brown v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1049.) Summary adjudication is denied for loss of consortium as Defendant argued only that it should be granted assuming there were no other valid causes of action. Finally, summary adjudication is denied for punitive damages. Plaintiff presented evidence that Defendant knew that the use of Kenalog in epidural injections was a widespread practice, knew that this could result in serious adverse neurologic injuries, and did nothing to prevent such injuries. (PAF 20-43.) A trier of fact could infer from these facts, if proven, that Defendant's conduct was malicious. Because several of these facts are disputed by Defendant, material issues of triable fact exist. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JKS)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS RUSH VS. WHITE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Treadwell & Rollo, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance, Testimony, And Production Of Documents From: 1) David Flaherty 2) J. Christopher Fleming, 3) Frank Mcguyer, 4) Michael Defelice, And 5) Dennis M Rush
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 29, 2014, Line 13, Defendants Treadwell & Rollo, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance, Testimony, And Production Of Documents From: 1) David Flaherty 2) J. Christopher Fleming, 3) Frank Mcguyer, 4) Michael Defelice, And 5) Dennis M Rush. Motion granted in part and denied in part. Motion to compel Plaintiffs Flaherty, Fleming, McGuyer, DeFelice, and Rush to appear for depositions on September 8, 2014 is granted with modification. Plaintiffs are ordered to appear on or before September 18, 2014 to provide the parties a reasonable opportunity to review documents. (Rosemont v. Sup. Ct. (1964) 60 Cal.2d 709.) Motion to compel production of documents at the depositions is granted on the condition that the trial is continued and the request for documents in the deposition notices is modified, striking "having any connection with" from the definition of "referring or relating" to. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SHARMAN SPECTOR VS. AUDREY SPECTOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 29, 2014, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer to causes of action 1-3 [breach of contract] are overruled. Cross-complainant sufficiently alleges the terms of the contracts according to their legal intendment and effect. Cross-complainant alleges the substance of the key terms of each contract and is not obliged to attach a copy of the written agreements to the cross-complaint. The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Cross-complainant has leave to plead the elements of the cause of action (see CACI 1902) with more particularity. The demurrer to the breach of fiduciary duty and declaratory relief causes of action are overruled. rulings.Case Title: THOMAS A. WARD VS. FLIESLER MEYER LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Seeking Court'S Permission To Conduct Discovery Of Defendants' Finance Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 29 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF THOMAS WARD Motion Seeking Court's Permission To Conduct Discovery Of Defendants' Finance Records. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion is DENIED. The Court does not find that Plaintiff has established that there is a substantial probability that he will prevail on the claim(s) pursuant to C.C.P. Section 3294 due to statute of limitations issues. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
